name: AoT Taxonomy
description: Versioned vocabulary for describing Human x AI (AoT) compositions. See taxonomy/README.md.

concepts.create: Produce a new artifact (draft, design, plan).
concepts.decide-support: Prepare a human decision without making it.
concepts.transform: Convert material between forms or formats.
concepts.monitor: Watch for conditions that need human attention.
concepts.learn: Improve the system itself from experience.

concepts.owner: The accountable human for the outcome.
concepts.contributor: A human providing material or expertise.

concepts.instruction: A prompt, brief, or task statement.
concepts.signal: An event or metric that triggers work.

concepts.domain-knowledge: Facts and expertise relevant to the domain.
concepts.history: What happened before: prior runs, decisions, incidents.

concepts.text-model: Language model for generation or analysis.
concepts.retrieval: Finding relevant material from a corpus.
concepts.automation: Connecting and triggering steps across tools.
concepts.transport: Moving information between people and systems.

concepts.extract: Pull structured items from unstructured material.
concepts.route: Send work to the right actor.
concepts.execute: Perform an operation on a system.
concepts.publish: Make an artifact available to its audience.

concepts.draft: Material requiring human revision before use.
concepts.decision-input: Material prepared for a human decision.

human_controls.question: Where can a human steer or stop?

concepts.checkpoint: A defined human review point work cannot pass alone.
concepts.approval: Explicit human sign-off required to proceed.
concepts.limit: A bound on what automation may do without a human.
concepts.override: A human's ability to countermand the system.
concepts.rollback: The documented way to undo a step.
concepts.disclosure: Telling affected people that AI was involved.

concepts.source-list: What material fed the work, with origins.
concepts.version-history: The sequence of changes to an artifact.
concepts.run-record: Inputs, outputs, and settings of a specific run.
concepts.review-record: Who reviewed, what they found, what they decided.
concepts.attribution: Which contributions were human and which AI.

concepts.fabrication: Confident output with no basis (citations, facts, data).
concepts.privacy-leak: Data shared beyond consent or policy.
concepts.bias: Systematic skew in analysis or generation.
concepts.over-automation: Removing human judgment where it was load-bearing.
concepts.provenance-loss: Inability to trace how an artifact came to be.
concepts.drift: Gradual divergence between the documented and actual system.

concepts.metrics: Quantitative signals about runs over time.
concepts.retro: A structured learning review after the work.
concepts.revision: Folding lessons back into the artifact, with a version bump.
concepts.retirement: Honestly ending an artifact that no longer serves.
